description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: South Dakota ( ; Siouan languages: Dakȟóta itókaga, pronounced [daˈkˣota iˈtokaga]) is a landlocked state in the Upper Midwest, North Central region of the United States.

description: 1941 Japanese surprise attack on the U.S
description_wiki: On December 7, 1941, the Empire of Japan launched a surprise military strike on the United States Pacific Fleet at its naval base at Pearl Harbor on Oahu, Hawaii.

description: United States Space Force Base located in Santa Barbara County, California
description_wiki: Vandenberg Space Force Base (IATA: VBG, ICAO: KVBG, FAA LID: VBG), previously Vandenberg Air Force Base, is a United States Space Force Base in Santa Barbara County, California.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: California () is a state in the Western United States that lies on the Pacific Coast.

description: 1941-1956 United States Army formation
description_wiki: The 5th Armored Division ("Victory") was an armored formation of the United States Army active from 1941 to 1945 and from 1950 to 1956.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Tennessee ( , locally ), officially the State of Tennessee, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

surface_forms: New York, and then you went to England
aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: country in north-west Europe, part of the United Kingdom
description_wiki: England is a country that is part of the United Kingdom.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Pennsylvania, officially the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, is a U.S. state located in the Mid-Atlantic, Northeastern, Appalachian, and Great Lakes regions of the United States.

description: province of Canada
description_wiki: Newfoundland and Labrador is the easternmost province of Canada, in the country's Atlantic region.

description: lightweight prefabricated structure
description_wiki: A Quonset hut  is a lightweight prefabricated structure of corrugated galvanized steel with a semi-circular cross-section.

description: holiday originating in Christianity, usually December 25
description_wiki: Christmas is an annual festival commemorating the birth of Jesus Christ, observed primarily on December 25 as a religious and cultural celebration among billions of people around the world.

description: country in Central Europe
description_wiki: Germany, officially the Federal Republic of Germany, is a country in Western and Central Europe.

description: country in Western Europe and other continents (through its overseas territories in America, Africa and Oceania)
description_wiki: France, officially the French Republic, is a country primarily located in Western Europe.

description: one of the four autonomous countries of the Kingdom of the Netherlands; with territories in the Caribbean
description_wiki: The Netherlands, informally Holland, is a country in Northwestern Europe, with overseas territories in the Caribbean.

description: geographical and cultural region which extends over a part of France and the British Channel Islands
description_wiki: Normandy is a geographical and cultural region in northwestern Europe, roughly coextensive with the historical Duchy of Normandy.

description: region of western Germany
description_wiki: The Rhineland (German: Rheinland [ˈʁaɪnlant] ; Dutch: Rijnland; Kölsch: Rhingland; Latin: Rhenania) is a loosely defined area of Western Germany along the Rhine, chiefly its middle section, sometimes encompassing parts of Belgium.

description: low mountain range in Belgium, France, Luxembourg and Germany
description_wiki: The Ardennes, also known as the Ardennes Forest or Forest of Ardennes, is a region of extensive forests, rough terrain, rolling hills and ridges primarily in Belgium and Luxembourg, extending into Germany and France.

description: region of Europe
description_wiki: Central Europe is a geographical region of Europe between Eastern, Southern, Western and Northern Europe.

description: United States Armed Forces decoration award
description_wiki: The Bronze Star Medal (BSM) is a United States Armed Forces decoration awarded to members of the United States Armed Forces for either heroic achievement, heroic service, meritorious achievement, or meritorious service in a combat zone.

description: United States military award
description_wiki: The Good Conduct Medal is one of the oldest military awards of the United States Armed Forces.

description: military decoration of the United States
1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: United States Army general (1885–1945)
description_wiki: George Smith Patton Jr.

description: semi-automatic pistol of German origin
largest Christian Church, made up of 24 autonomous Churches in communion with the Pope, part of the Latin Church aka Latin Christianity aka western Christianity
description_wiki: The Catholic Church (Latin: Ecclesia Catholica), commonly called the Roman Catholic Church, is the largest Christian church, with an estimated 1.28 to 1.41 billion baptized members worldwide as of 2026.
